persona.expertise: TypeScript, MCP Protocol, Playwright Traces, R2/S3 Storage, JSON Processing

task.objective: Implement MCP tools that allow Claude to read test artifacts (logs, screenshots, traces)
task.success_criteria: Two functional MCP tools that securely fetch and analyze test artifacts, removing the need for users to manually copy-paste error logs.

context.background: The Exolar QA dashboard stores test artifacts in Cloudflare R2. Currently, users must manually find and paste log contents when asking for debugging help. These tools will allow Claude to self-serve the context needed to diagnose test failures.

existing_infrastructure.storage: Cloudflare R2 bucket for artifacts (videos, traces, screenshots, logs)
existing_infrastructure.database: Neon PostgreSQL with test_artifacts table linking to R2 URLs
existing_infrastructure.auth: Organization-based multi-tenancy with API key authentication

functional.requirement: Fetch raw text content of log files or metadata/URL of binary files
Extract meaningful error steps from Playwright Trace files
Validate artifact URLs belong to the organization's allowed domains
Support max_lines parameter to limit returned content size

non_functional.requirement: Security: Validate URLs against allowed R2 bucket domains
Performance: Stream large files, don't load entirely into memory
Reliability: Graceful handling of missing or corrupted artifacts

tool.name: get_artifact_content
tool.purpose: Fetches the raw text content of a log file or the metadata/URL of a binary file

input_schema.field: The artifact URL returned by get_execution_details
Maximum number of lines to return for text files

implementation_logic.step: Validate URL belongs to organization's R2 bucket or allowed domains
Determine file type via extension or Content-Type header

Fetch content based on type: - Text/Logs: Download and return last max_lines - Images: Return signed URL (or description if vision supported) - Traces: Return message "Use analyze_failure_trace instead"
Verify authenticated user has access to the signed URL

output.success: File content (text) or signed URL (binary) with metadata
output.error: Error message explaining why content could not be retrieved

tool.name: analyze_failure_trace
tool.purpose: Extracts meaningful error steps from a Playwright Trace file without downloading the entire zip

input_schema.field: The URL of the Playwright trace zip file
The execution ID for context and validation

implementation_logic.step: Stream download the trace zip file
Extract trace.network or trace.actions from the zip
Filter and extract: - Failed actions (red steps) - Console errors - Network requests with 4xx/5xx status codes

output.success: JSON summary containing: - failed_actions: Array of failed step descriptions - console_errors: Array of console error messages - failed_requests: Array of failed network requests with status codes - summary: Human-readable failure summary
output.error: Error message if trace cannot be parsed

example.user_query: Why did the login test fail?

agent_steps.step: Calls get_execution_details for the failed execution
Reads "401 Unauthorized - API key expired" in log
Explains: "The test failed because the API key has expired"

example.description: Analyzing a Playwright trace for failure details

example.output:
{ "type": "text", "content": "[2025-01-03T10:15:30Z] Starting test: user-login.spec.ts
[2025-01-03T10:15:31Z] Navigating to /login
[2025-01-03T10:15:32Z] ERROR: Network timeout after 30000ms
...", "total_lines": 847, "returned_lines": 100, "truncated": true }

security.rule: NEVER fetch URLs outside the organization's configured R2 bucket domain
Validate execution_id belongs to the authenticated organization
Use signed URLs with short expiration times (15 minutes max)

performance.rule: Stream large files instead of loading into memory
Default max_lines to 500 to prevent context overflow

avoid.item: Downloading entire trace zips into memory
Returning binary content directly (use URLs instead)
Exposing internal file paths or server details in errors

output_format.code: TypeScript implementation files to add to packages/mcp-server/src/: - tools/get-artifact-content.ts - tools/analyze-failure-trace.ts - utils/url-validator.ts - utils/trace-parser.ts
output_format.tests: Unit tests for URL validation and trace parsing logic
output_format.documentation: Update packages/mcp-server/README.md with new tool documentation

roi_analysis.time_saved: High - Eliminates "Can you paste the log?" roundtrips
roi_analysis.autonomy: High - Enables "Fix this build" autonomous debugging loops
roi_analysis.complexity: Medium - Trace parsing is non-trivial but well-documented
